description: Generate pseudo-random numbers. Implements pseudo-random number generators for various distributions.

functions.name: seed

params.name: x
params.description: Seed value. If omitted or None, the current system time is used. If x is an int or long, it is used directly.

returns.description: No return value

functions.description: Initializes the random number generator. If x is omitted or None, the current system time is used. If x is an int or long, it is used directly as the seed.

functions.name: random

returns.description: A random floating-point number in the range [0.0, 1.0)

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number N such that 0.0 <= N < 1.0.

functions.name: uniform

params.name: b
params.description: Upper bound (may be inclusive depending on rounding)

returns.description: A random floating-point number N such that a <= N <= b (or b <= N <= a)

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number N such that a <= N <= b for a <= b and b <= N <= a for b < a. The end-point value b may or may not be included depending on floating-point rounding.

functions.name: randint

returns.description: A random integer N such that a <= N <= b

functions.description: Returns a random integer N such that a <= N <= b. Both endpoints are included.

functions.name: randrange

returns.description: A randomly selected element from range(start, stop, step)

functions.description: Returns a randomly selected element from range(start, stop, step). Equivalent to choice(range(start, stop, step)) but does not build the full range object.

functions.name: choice

params.name: seq
params.description: A non-empty sequence (list, tuple, string, etc.)

returns.description: A random element from the sequence

functions.description: Returns a random element from the non-empty sequence seq. Raises IndexError if seq is empty.

functions.name: shuffle

params.name: x
params.description: The list to shuffle in place

params.name: random
params.description: Optional zero-argument function returning a random float in [0.0, 1.0). Defaults to the random() function.

returns.description: No return value; the list is modified in place

functions.description: Shuffles the list x in place. The optional argument random is a zero-argument function returning a random float in [0.0, 1.0); by default, this is the module's random() function.

functions.name: sample

params.name: k
params.description: Number of unique elements to choose

returns.description: A new list containing k unique elements chosen from the population

functions.description: Returns a k-length list of unique elements chosen from the population sequence or set. Used for random sampling without replacement. The original population is left unchanged.

functions.name: getrandbits

params.name: k
params.description: Number of random bits to generate

returns.description: A Python long integer with k random bits

functions.description: Returns a Python long integer with k random bits. Useful for methods like randrange() that handle arbitrarily large ranges.

functions.name: gauss

returns.description: A random number from the Gaussian distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from a Gaussian (normal) distribution with mean mu and standard deviation sigma. Slightly faster than normalvariate() but not thread-safe.

functions.name: normalvariate

returns.description: A random number from the normal distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from a normal (Gaussian) distribution with mean mu and standard deviation sigma. Thread-safe alternative to gauss().

functions.name: expovariate

params.name: lambd
params.description: The rate parameter (1.0 / desired mean). Must be nonzero.

returns.description: A random number from the exponential distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from an exponential distribution. lambd is 1.0 divided by the desired mean. Return values range from 0 to positive infinity if lambd is positive, or from negative infinity to 0 if lambd is negative.

functions.name: triangular

params.name: mode
params.description: The mode (peak) of the distribution. Default is midpoint of low and high.

returns.description: A random number from the triangular distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number N such that low <= N <= high with the specified mode between those bounds. The mode defaults to the midpoint between low and high.

functions.name: betavariate

returns.description: A random number in the range [0, 1] from the Beta distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from the Beta distribution. Conditions on the parameters are alpha > 0 and beta > 0. Returned values range between 0 and 1.

functions.name: gammavariate

returns.description: A random number from the Gamma distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from the Gamma distribution. Conditions on the parameters are alpha > 0 and beta > 0. The probability distribution function is: pdf(x) = (x**(alpha-1) * exp(-x/beta)) / (gamma(alpha) * beta**alpha).

functions.name: paretovariate

returns.description: A random number from the Pareto distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from the Pareto distribution with shape parameter alpha.

functions.name: weibullvariate

returns.description: A random number from the Weibull distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from the Weibull distribution with scale parameter alpha and shape parameter beta.

functions.name: vonmisesvariate

params.name: kappa
params.description: Concentration parameter (>= 0). When kappa is 0, the distribution is uniform over 0 to 2*pi.

returns.description: A random angle in radians from the von Mises distribution

functions.description: Returns a random floating-point number from the von Mises distribution (circular normal distribution). mu is the mean angle in radians, and kappa is the concentration parameter.

classes.name: Random
classes.description: Class that implements the default pseudo-random number generator. All module-level functions (random(), randint(), choice(), etc.) are bound methods of a hidden instance of this class. You can instantiate your own Random objects to get independent generators.

params.name: x
params.description: Optional seed value. If omitted, the current system time is used.

constructors.description: Creates a new Random instance, optionally seeded with x.

methods.name: seed

params.name: x
params.description: Seed value. If omitted or None, the current system time is used.

returns.description: No return value

methods.description: Initializes the internal state of the random number generator.

methods.name: getstate

returns.description: An object capturing the current internal state of the generator

methods.description: Returns an object capturing the current internal state of the generator. This object can be passed to setstate() to restore the state.

methods.name: setstate

params.name: state
params.description: A state object as returned by getstate()

returns.description: No return value

methods.description: Restores the internal state of the generator to the state returned by a previous call to getstate().

methods.name: jumpahead

returns.description: No return value

methods.description: Changes the internal state to one that is likely far away from the current state. n is a non-negative integer used to scramble the current state. Useful for multi-threaded programs.

methods.name: random

returns.description: A random float in [0.0, 1.0)

methods.description: Returns the next random floating-point number in the range [0.0, 1.0).

methods.name: uniform

returns.description: A random float N where a <= N <= b

methods.description: Returns a random floating-point number N such that a <= N <= b.

methods.name: randint

returns.description: A random integer N where a <= N <= b

methods.description: Returns a random integer N such that a <= N <= b.

methods.name: choice

returns.description: A random element from the sequence

methods.description: Returns a random element from the non-empty sequence.

methods.name: shuffle

params.name: x
params.description: The list to shuffle in place

params.name: random
params.description: Optional random function returning float in [0.0, 1.0)

returns.description: No return value; list is modified in place

methods.description: Shuffles the list x in place.

methods.name: sample

returns.description: A list of k unique elements

methods.description: Returns a k-length list of unique elements chosen from the population.

classes.name: SystemRandom
classes.description: Class that uses os.urandom() to generate random numbers from sources provided by the operating system. Not all methods are available (notably getstate() and setstate() raise NotImplementedError). Use this when cryptographic-quality randomness is needed.

params.name: x
params.description: Optional seed value. Ignored by SystemRandom since entropy comes from the OS.

constructors.description: Creates a new SystemRandom instance. The seed argument is ignored; entropy comes from the operating system.

methods.name: random

returns.description: A random float in [0.0, 1.0) from OS entropy

methods.description: Returns the next random floating-point number using OS-supplied entropy.

methods.name: getrandbits

returns.description: A long integer with k random bits

methods.description: Returns a long integer with k random bits, sourced from the OS.
